violations.text: Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies was not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Thermometers are lacking in the walk-in cooler. All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The door to the outside, located at the back of the establishment, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by areas of droppings observed. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Areas of droppings must be properly cleaned in order to observe new activity and increase pest control as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets of the pizza prep unit were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gaskets must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items/clutter observed in the storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment, litter, and clutter to prevent harborage conditions. Progress has been made. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024

Sliced deli meats, chicken, soups observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed. No foods/food equipment observed to be adulterated. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Areas of droppings are to be properly cleaned in order to observe new activity and increase pest control as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the pizza prep unit observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed under the soda storage shelving. No foods observed to be adulterated. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Droppings must be cleaned up in order to check for new activity and increase pest control as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2023

The cutting boards of prep units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of some of the equipment and gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
